skill.name: frontend-fundamentals
skill.description: Reviews frontend component code for architecture, state management, performance, accessibility, and common UI anti-patterns.
skill.summary: Guides React, Vue, and Svelte component reviews with practical frontend quality checks.
skill.author: DanielPodolsky

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported Markdown backticks as shell execution and flagged unrelated text as weak cryptography. Manual review found only frontend review guidance, checklist items, and non-executable code examples, with no prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Reported as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution across Markdown example blocks. The cited lines show frontend component examples and JSX snippets, not executable shell commands or Ruby interpolation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched content is inside Markdown fenced examples and contains frontend review examples. No command invocation, subprocess API, or instruction to execute commands is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at the cited lines. Manual review found skill metadata, a review quote, and a naming checklist item, with no cryptographic API or algorithm usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose about frontend component review. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or any other cryptographic operation.

content.value_statement: Frontend reviews often miss architecture, state, and accessibility issues until code becomes hard to maintain. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a focused checklist for practical component review.
actual_capabilities: Reviews component responsibility, size, naming, and prop count.
Checks state placement, prop drilling, context use, and server state separation.
Identifies performance concerns such as unnecessary renders and missing memoization.
Prompts accessibility checks for semantic HTML, ARIA, and keyboard navigation.
Provides Socratic review questions for mentoring junior frontend developers.
limitations: Does not run automated tests, linters, or accessibility scanners.
Does not include framework-specific rules beyond common frontend patterns.
Does not inspect referenced standards unless those files are available.
May need project context to judge tradeoffs in complex component designs.

use_cases.title: Review a Pull Request
use_cases.description: Apply a concise checklist to component code before merging a frontend change.

use_cases.title: Coach Component Design
use_cases.description: Use guided questions to help a junior developer improve component boundaries and state placement.

use_cases.title: Audit UI Maintainability
use_cases.description: Find large components, prop drilling, render logic, and accessibility gaps in an existing UI.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Component Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React component using frontend fundamentals. Focus on one responsibility, prop count, state placement, and accessibility.

prompt_templates.title: State Management Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of data flow and state ownership.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these frontend components for state management issues. Identify misplaced state, prop drilling, context misuse, and server state concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Performance and Rendering Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this UI code for unnecessary renders, expensive render logic, missing memoization, callback churn, and opportunities for code splitting.

prompt_templates.title: Mentoring Review Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request as a frontend mentor. Ask Socratic questions first, then list concrete issues and suggested refactors.

output_examples.input: A dashboard component fetches data, manages filters, renders charts, and handles routing.
output: The review would flag the component as doing too many jobs.
It would suggest separating the page container, chart presentation, filter controls, and data hook.
It would ask which parts should be reusable outside the dashboard page.

output_examples.input: A widget receives the same user prop through App, Layout, Main, and Widget.
output: The review would identify prop drilling beyond the recommended depth.
It would suggest moving shared user access to a closer provider or custom hook.
It would ask which components actually need the user data.

output_examples.input: A list filters active users directly inside render and maps the result immediately.
output: The review would flag repeated work during render.
It would recommend deriving active users outside the JSX with memoization when the list is expensive.
It would check whether the optimization is justified by the data size.

best_practices: Provide enough surrounding component code to judge responsibility and data flow.
Ask for separate sections on architecture, state, performance, and accessibility.
Use the Socratic questions when the review is meant for mentoring.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the checklist as a replacement for project-specific design standards.
Do not request broad frontend rewrites without explaining the product goal.
Do not apply memoization rules mechanically when render work is trivial.

faq.question: Which frontend frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: It targets React, Vue, and Svelte component reviews, with examples focused on common component patterns.

faq.question: Does it execute code or run tools?
faq.answer: No. The skill is Markdown guidance for review behavior and does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Can it replace automated accessibility testing?
faq.answer: No. It prompts accessibility review, but scanners and manual keyboard testing are still needed.

faq.question: Is it useful for junior developers?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes questions that help mentors guide developers toward better frontend decisions.

faq.question: Does it enforce exact component size limits?
faq.answer: It uses limits such as component length and prop count as review signals, not absolute rules.

faq.question: Can it review server-side application code?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for frontend components, UI rendering, state management, styling, and routing.
